title: The results of a state audit released today show the lack of accountability of billions of dollars thrown at the homeless crisis in California. Senator Rosilicie Ochoa Bogh (R-Yuca

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California has spent more than $24 billion on homelessness over the past five years.",
    "The population of unhoused Californians has grown to more than 181,000.",
    "The state has not tracked and reported on the State’s funding for homelessness programs since its 2023 assessment covering fiscal years 2018–19 through 2020–21.",
    "The state currently has no plans to perform a similar assessment of homelessness funding in the future.",
    "In the absence of an up-to-date assessment, the State and its policymakers are likely to struggle to understand homelessness programs’ ongoing costs and achieved outcomes.",
    "The state has not established a consistent method for gathering information on homelessness programs’ costs and outcomes.",
    "As a result of not having a consistent method for gathering information, the State lacks information that would allow it to make data-driven policy decisions and identify gaps in services.",
    "The state has neither ensured the accuracy of the information in the state data system, nor has it used this information to evaluate the success of homelessness programs."
  ]
}
```
